NKJV.1: On the third day there was a wedding in Cana of Galilee, and the mother of Jesus was there.
NKJV.2: Now both Jesus and His disciples were invited to the wedding.
NKJV.3: And when they ran out of wine, the mother of Jesus said to Him, “They have no wine.”
NKJV.4: Jesus said to her, “Woman,  what does your concern have to do with Me?  My hour has not yet come.”
NKJV.5: His mother said to the servants, “Whatever He says to you, do it.”
NKJV.6: Now there were set there six waterpots of stone, according to the manner of purification of the Jews, containing twenty or thirty gallons apiece.
NKJV.7: Jesus said to them, “Fill the waterpots with water.” And they filled them up to the brim.
NKJV.8: And He said to them, “Draw some out now, and take it to the master of the feast.” And they took it.
NKJV.9: When the master of the feast had tasted the water that was made wine, and did not know where it came from (but the servants who had drawn the water knew), the master of the feast called the bridegroom.
NKJV.10: And he said to him, “Every man at the beginning sets out the good wine, and when the guests have well drunk, then the inferior. You have kept the good wine until now!”
NKJV.11: This beginning of signs Jesus did in Cana of Galilee, and manifested His glory; and His disciples believed in Him.
NKJV.12: After this He went down to Capernaum, He, His mother, His brothers, and His disciples; and they did not stay there many days.
NKJV.13: Now the Passover of the Jews was at hand, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
NKJV.14: And He found in the temple those who sold oxen and sheep and doves, and the money changers doing business.
NKJV.15: When He had made a whip of cords, He drove them all out of the temple, with the sheep and the oxen, and poured out the changers’ money and overturned the tables.
NKJV.16: And He said to those who sold doves, “Take these things away! Do not make  My Father’s house a house of merchandise!”
NKJV.17: Then His disciples remembered that it was written, “Zeal for Your house has eaten Me up.”
NKJV.18: So the Jews answered and said to Him, “What sign do You show to us, since You do these things?”
NKJV.19: Jesus answered and said to them, “Destroy this temple, and in three days I will raise it up.”
NKJV.20: Then the Jews said, “It has taken forty-six years to build this temple, and will You raise it up in three days?”
NKJV.21: But He was speaking of the temple of His body.
NKJV.22: Therefore, when He had risen from the dead, His disciples remembered that He had said this to them; and they believed the Scripture and the word which Jesus had said.
NKJV.23: Now when He was in Jerusalem at the Passover, during the feast, many believed in His name when they saw the signs which He did.
NKJV.24: But Jesus did not commit Himself to them, because He knew all men,
NKJV.25: and had no need that anyone should testify of man, for He knew what was in man.

ESV.1: On the third day there was a wedding at Cana in Galilee, and the mother of Jesus was there.
ESV.2: Jesus also was invited to the wedding with his disciples.
ESV.3: When the wine ran out, the mother of Jesus said to him, “They have no wine.”
ESV.4: And Jesus said to her,  “Woman, what does this have to do with me? My hour has not yet come.”
ESV.5: His mother said to the servants, “Do whatever he tells you.”
ESV.6: Now there were six stone water jars there for the Jewish rites of purification, each holding twenty or thirty gallons.
ESV.7: Jesus said to the servants, “Fill the jars with water.” And they filled them up to the brim.
ESV.8: And he said to them, “Now draw some out and take it to the master of the feast.” So they took it.
ESV.9: When the master of the feast tasted the water now become wine, and did not know where it came from (though the servants who had drawn the water knew), the master of the feast called the bridegroom
ESV.10: and said to him, “Everyone serves the good wine first, and when people have drunk freely, then the poor wine. But you have kept the good wine until now.”
ESV.11: This, the first of his signs, Jesus did at Cana in Galilee, and manifested his glory. And his disciples believed in him.
ESV.12: After this he went down to Capernaum, with his mother and his brothers  and his disciples, and they stayed there for a few days.
ESV.13: The Passover of the Jews was at hand, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
ESV.14: In the temple he found those who were selling oxen and sheep and pigeons, and the money-changers sitting there.
ESV.15: And making a whip of cords, he drove them all out of the temple, with the sheep and oxen. And he poured out the coins of the money-changers and overturned their tables.
ESV.16: And he told those who sold the pigeons, “Take these things away; do not make my Father's house a house of trade.”
ESV.17: His disciples remembered that it was written, “Zeal for your house will consume me.”
ESV.18: So the Jews said to him, “What sign do you show us for doing these things?”
ESV.19: Jesus answered them,  “Destroy this temple, and in three days I will raise it up.”
ESV.20: The Jews then said, “It has taken forty-six years to build this temple,  and will you raise it up in three days?”
ESV.21: But he was speaking about the temple of his body.
ESV.22: When therefore he was raised from the dead, his disciples remembered that he had said this, and they believed the Scripture and the word that Jesus had spoken.
ESV.23: Now when he was in Jerusalem at the Passover Feast, many believed in his name when they saw the signs that he was doing.
ESV.24: But Jesus on his part did not entrust himself to them, because he knew all people
ESV.25: and needed no one to bear witness about man, for he himself knew what was in man.

NASB.1: On the third day there was a wedding in Cana of Galilee, and the mother of Jesus was there;
NASB.2: and both Jesus and His disciples were invited to the wedding.
NASB.3: When the wine ran out, the mother of Jesus said to Him, 'They have no wine.'
NASB.4: And Jesus said to her, 'Woman, what does that have to do with us? My hour has not yet come.'
NASB.5: His mother said to the servants, 'Whatever He says to you, do it.'
NASB.6: Now there were six stone waterpots set there for the Jewish custom of purification, containing twenty or thirty gallons each.
NASB.7: Jesus said to them, 'Fill the waterpots with water.' So they filled them up to the brim.
NASB.8: And He said to them, 'Draw [some] out now and take it to the headwaiter.' So they took it [to him].
NASB.9: When the headwaiter tasted the water which had become wine, and did not know where it came from (but the servants who had drawn the water knew), the headwaiter called the bridegroom,
NASB.10: and said to him, 'Every man serves the good wine first, and when [the people] have drunk freely, [then he serves] the poorer [wine]; [but] you have kept the good wine until now.'
NASB.11: This beginning of [His] signs Jesus did in Cana of Galilee, and manifested His glory, and His disciples believed in Him.
NASB.12: After this He went down to Capernaum, He and His mother and [His] brothers and His disciples; and they stayed there a few days.
NASB.13: The Passover of the Jews was near, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
NASB.14: And He found in the temple those who were selling oxen and sheep and doves, and the money changers seated [at their tables].
NASB.15: And He made a scourge of cords, and drove [them] all out of the temple, with the sheep and the oxen; and He poured out the coins of the money changers and overturned their tables;
NASB.16: and to those who were selling the doves He said, 'Take these things away; stop making My Father's house a place of business.'
NASB.17: His disciples remembered that it was written, 'ZEAL FOR YOUR HOUSE WILL CONSUME ME.'
NASB.18: The Jews then said to Him, 'What sign do You show us as your authority for doing these things?'
NASB.19: Jesus answered them, 'Destroy this temple, and in three days I will raise it up.'
NASB.20: The Jews then said, 'It took forty-six years to build this temple, and will You raise it up in three days?'
NASB.21: But He was speaking of the temple of His body.
NASB.22: So when He was raised from the dead, His disciples remembered that He said this; and they believed the Scripture and the word which Jesus had spoken.
NASB.23: Now when He was in Jerusalem at the Passover, during the feast, many believed in His name, observing His signs which He was doing.
NASB.24: But Jesus, on His part, was not entrusting Himself to them, for He knew all men,
NASB.25: and because He did not need anyone to testify concerning man, for He Himself knew what was in man.

NLT.1: The next day there was a wedding celebration in the village of Cana in Galilee. Jesus’ mother was there,
NLT.2: and Jesus and his disciples were also invited to the celebration.
NLT.3: The wine supply ran out during the festivities, so Jesus’ mother told him, “They have no more wine.”
NLT.4: “Dear woman, that’s not our problem,” Jesus replied. “My time has not yet come.”
NLT.5: But his mother told the servants, “Do whatever he tells you.”
NLT.6: Standing nearby were six stone water jars, used for Jewish ceremonial washing. Each could hold twenty to thirty gallons.
NLT.7: Jesus told the servants, “Fill the jars with water.” When the jars had been filled,
NLT.8: he said, “Now dip some out, and take it to the master of ceremonies.” So the servants followed his instructions.
NLT.9: When the master of ceremonies tasted the water that was now wine, not knowing where it had come from (though, of course, the servants knew), he called the bridegroom over.
NLT.10: “A host always serves the best wine first,” he said. “Then, when everyone has had a lot to drink, he brings out the less expensive wine. But you have kept the best until now!”
NLT.11: This miraculous sign at Cana in Galilee was the first time Jesus revealed his glory. And his disciples believed in him.
NLT.12: After the wedding he went to Capernaum for a few days with his mother, his brothers, and his disciples.
NLT.13: It was nearly time for the Jewish Passover celebration, so Jesus went to Jerusalem.
NLT.14: In the Temple area he saw merchants selling cattle, sheep, and doves for sacrifices; he also saw dealers at tables exchanging foreign money.
NLT.15: Jesus made a whip from some ropes and chased them all out of the Temple. He drove out the sheep and cattle, scattered the money changers’ coins over the floor, and turned over their tables.
NLT.16: Then, going over to the people who sold doves, he told them, “Get these things out of here. Stop turning my Father’s house into a marketplace!”
NLT.17: Then his disciples remembered this prophecy from the Scriptures: “Passion for God’s house will consume me.”
NLT.18: But the Jewish leaders demanded, “What are you doing? If God gave you authority to do this, show us a miraculous sign to prove it.”
NLT.19: “All right,” Jesus replied. “Destroy this temple, and in three days I will raise it up.”
NLT.20: “What!” they exclaimed. “It has taken forty-six years to build this Temple, and you can rebuild it in three days?”
NLT.21: But when Jesus said “this temple,” he meant his own body.
NLT.22: After he was raised from the dead, his disciples remembered he had said this, and they believed both the Scriptures and what Jesus had said.
NLT.23: Because of the miraculous signs Jesus did in Jerusalem at the Passover celebration, many began to trust in him.
NLT.24: But Jesus didn’t trust them, because he knew all about people.
NLT.25: No one needed to tell him about human nature, for he knew what was in each person’s heart.

WEB.1: The third day, there was a marriage in Cana of Galilee. Jesus’ mother was there.
WEB.2: Jesus also was invited, with his disciples, to the marriage.
WEB.3: When the wine ran out, Jesus’ mother said to him, “They have no wine.”
WEB.4: Jesus said to her, “Woman, what does that have to do with you and me? My hour has not yet come.”
WEB.5: His mother said to the servants, “Whatever he says to you, do it.”
WEB.6: Now there were six water pots of stone set there after the Jews’ way of purifying, containing two or three metretes  apiece.
WEB.7: Jesus said to them, “Fill the water pots with water.” They filled them up to the brim.
WEB.8: He said to them, “Now draw some out, and take it to the ruler of the feast.” So they took it.
WEB.9: When the ruler of the feast tasted the water now become wine, and didn’t know where it came from (but the servants who had drawn the water knew), the ruler of the feast called the bridegroom,
WEB.10: and said to him, “Everyone serves the good wine first, and when the guests have drunk freely, then that which is worse. You have kept the good wine until now!”
WEB.11: This beginning of his signs Jesus did in Cana of Galilee, and revealed his glory; and his disciples believed in him.
WEB.12: After this, he went down to Capernaum, he, and his mother, his brothers, and his disciples; and they stayed there a few days.
WEB.13: The Passover of the Jews was at hand, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
WEB.14: He found in the temple those who sold oxen, sheep, and doves, and the changers of money sitting.
WEB.15: He made a whip of cords, and threw all out of the temple, both the sheep and the oxen; and he poured out the changers’ money, and overthrew their tables.
WEB.16: To those who sold the doves, he said, “Take these things out of here! Don’t make my Father’s house a marketplace!”
WEB.17: His disciples remembered that it was written, “Zeal for your house will eat me up.”
WEB.18: The Jews therefore answered him, “What sign do you show us, seeing that you do these things?”
WEB.19: Jesus answered them, “Destroy this temple, and in three days I will raise it up.”
WEB.20: The Jews therefore said, “It took forty-six years to build this temple! Will you raise it up in three days?”
WEB.21: But he spoke of the temple of his body.
WEB.22: When therefore he was raised from the dead, his disciples remembered that he said this, and they believed the Scripture, and the word which Jesus had said.
WEB.23: Now when he was in Jerusalem at the Passover, during the feast, many believed in his name, observing his signs which he did.
WEB.24: But Jesus didn’t trust himself to them, because he knew everyone,
WEB.25: and because he didn’t need for anyone to testify concerning man; for he himself knew what was in man.

CSB17.1: On the third day a wedding took place in Cana <sup>ⓚ</sup> of Galilee. <sup>ⓛ</sup> Jesus’s mother <sup>ⓜ</sup> was there, and
CSB17.2: Jesus and his disciples were invited to the wedding as well.
CSB17.3: When the wine ran out, Jesus’s mother told him, “They don’t have any wine.”
CSB17.4: “What does that have to do with you and me, <sup>[1]</sup> <sup>ⓝ</sup> woman?” <sup>ⓐ</sup> Jesus asked. “My hour has not yet come.”
CSB17.5: “Do whatever he tells you,” his mother told the servants.
CSB17.6: Now six stone water jars had been set there for Jewish purification. <sup>ⓑ</sup> Each contained twenty or thirty gallons. <sup>[2]</sup>
CSB17.7: “Fill the jars with water,” Jesus told them. So they filled them to the brim.
CSB17.8: Then he said to them, “Now draw some out and take it to the headwaiter.” <sup>[3]</sup> And they did.
CSB17.9: When the headwaiter tasted the water (after it had become wine), he did not know where it came from — though the servants who had drawn the water knew. He called the groom
CSB17.10: and told him, “Everyone sets out the fine wine first, then, after people are drunk, the inferior. But you have kept the fine wine until now.”
CSB17.11: Jesus did this, the first of his signs, in Cana <sup>ⓒ</sup> of Galilee. <sup>ⓓ</sup> He revealed <sup>ⓔ</sup> his glory, <sup>ⓕ</sup> and his disciples believed in him.
CSB17.12: After this, he went down to Capernaum, <sup>ⓖ</sup> together with his mother, his brothers, <sup>ⓗ</sup> and his disciples, and they stayed there only a few days.
CSB17.13: The Jewish Passover <sup>ⓘ</sup> was near, and so Jesus went up to Jerusalem. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.14: In the temple <sup>ⓚ</sup> he found people selling oxen, sheep, and doves, and he also found the money changers sitting there.
CSB17.15: After making a whip out of cords, <sup>ⓛ</sup> he drove everyone out of the temple with their sheep <sup>ⓜ</sup> and oxen. He also poured out the money changers’ coins and overturned the tables.
CSB17.16: He told those who were selling doves, “Get these things out of here! Stop turning my Father’s <sup>ⓝ</sup>house <sup>ⓐ</sup>into a marketplace!” <sup>[4]</sup> <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.17: And his disciples remembered that it is written: Zeal ⓒ for your house will consume ⓓ me. [5] ⓔ
CSB17.18: So the Jews replied to him, “What sign will you show us for doing these things?”
CSB17.19: Jesus answered, “Destroy this temple, <sup>[6]</sup> <sup>ⓕ</sup>and I will raise it up in three days.” <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.20: Therefore the Jews said, “This temple took forty-six years to build, <sup>[7]</sup> and will you raise it up in three days?” <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.21: But he was speaking about the temple of his body. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.22: So when he was raised from the dead, <sup>ⓙ</sup> his disciples remembered that he had said this, <sup>ⓚ</sup> and they believed the Scripture <sup>ⓛ</sup> and the statement Jesus had made.
CSB17.23: While he was in Jerusalem <sup>ⓜ</sup> during the Passover Festival, <sup>ⓝ</sup> many believed in his name <sup>ⓐ</sup> when they saw the signs he was doing.
CSB17.24: Jesus, however, would not entrust himself to them, since he knew them all
CSB17.25: and because he did not need anyone to testify about man; for he himself knew what was in man. <sup>ⓑ</sup>

AMP.1: On the third day there was a wedding at Cana of Galilee, and the mother of Jesus was there;
AMP.2: and both Jesus and His disciples were invited to the wedding.
AMP.3: When the wine was all gone, the mother of Jesus said to Him, " They have no more wine."
AMP.4: Jesus said to her, "[Dear] woman, what is that to you and to Me? My time [to act and to be revealed] has not yet come."
AMP.5: His mother said to the servants, "Whatever He says to you, do it."
AMP.6: Now there were six stone waterpots set there for the Jewish custom of purification (ceremonial washing), containing twenty or thirty gallons each.
AMP.7: Jesus said to the servants, "Fill the waterpots with water." So they filled them up to the brim.
AMP.8: Then He said to them, "Draw some out now and take it to the headwaiter [of the banquet]." So they took it to him.
AMP.9: And when the headwaiter tasted the water which had turned into wine, not knowing where it came from (though the servants who had drawn the water knew) he called the bridegroom,
AMP.10: and said to him, "Everyone else serves his best wine first, and when people have drunk freely, then he serves that which is not so good; but you have kept back the good wine until now."
AMP.11: This, the first of His signs (attesting miracles), Jesus did in Cana of Galilee, and revealed His glory [displaying His deity and His great power openly], and His disciples believed [confidently] in Him [as the Messiah—they adhered to, trusted in, and relied on Him].
AMP.12: After this He went down to Capernaum, He and His mother and brothers and His disciples; and they stayed there a few days.
AMP.13: Now the Passover of the Jews was approaching, so Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
AMP.14: And in the temple [enclosure] He found the people who were selling oxen and sheep and doves, and the money changers sitting at their tables.
AMP.15: He made a whip of cords, and drove them all out of the temple, with the sheep and the oxen; and He scattered the coins of the money changers and overturned their tables;
AMP.16: then to those who sold the doves He said, "Take these things away! Stop making My Father's house a place of commerce!"
AMP.17: His disciples remembered that it is written [in the Scriptures], "Zeal (love, concern) for Your house [and its honor] will consume Me."
AMP.18: Then the Jews retorted, "What sign (attesting miracle) can You show us as [proof of] your authority for doing these things?"
AMP.19: Jesus answered them, "Destroy this temple, and in three days I will raise it up."
AMP.20: Then the Jews replied, "It took forty-six years to build this temple, and You will raise it up in three days?"
AMP.21: But He was speaking of the temple which was His body.
AMP.22: So when He had risen from the dead, His disciples remembered what He had said. And they believed and trusted in and relied on the Scripture and the words that Jesus had spoken.
AMP.23: Now when He was in Jerusalem at the Passover feast, many believed in His name [identifying themselves with Him] after seeing His signs (attesting miracles) which He was doing.
AMP.24: But Jesus, for His part, did not entrust Himself to them, because He knew all people [and understood the superficiality and fickleness of human nature],
AMP.25: and He did not need anyone to testify concerning man [and human nature], for He Himself knew what was in man [in their hearts—in the very core of their being].

MSG.1: Three days later there was a wedding in the village of Cana in Galilee. Jesus' mother was there.
MSG.2: Jesus and his disciples were guests also.
MSG.3: When they started running low on wine at the wedding banquet, Jesus' mother told him, "They're just about out of wine."
MSG.4: Jesus said, "Is that any of our business, Mother—yours or mine? This isn't my time. Don't push me."
MSG.5: She went ahead anyway, telling the servants, "Whatever he tells you, do it."
MSG.6: Six stoneware water pots were there, used by the Jews for ritual washings. Each held twenty to thirty gallons.
MSG.7: Jesus ordered the servants, "Fill the pots with water." And they filled them to the brim.
MSG.8: "Now fill your pitchers and take them to the host," Jesus said, and they did.
MSG.9: When the host tasted the water that had become wine (he didn't know what had just happened but the servants, of course, knew), he called out to the bridegroom,
MSG.10: "Everybody I know begins with their finest wines and after the guests have had their fill brings in the cheap stuff. But you've saved the best till now!"
MSG.11: This act in Cana of Galilee was the first sign Jesus gave, the first glimpse of his glory. And his disciples believed in him.
MSG.12: After this he went down to Capernaum along with his mother, brothers, and disciples, and stayed several days.
MSG.13: When the Passover Feast, celebrated each spring by the Jews, was about to take place, Jesus traveled up to Jerusalem.
MSG.14: He found the Temple teeming with people selling cattle and sheep and doves. The loan sharks were also there in full strength.
MSG.15: Jesus put together a whip out of strips of leather and chased them out of the Temple, stampeding the sheep and cattle, upending the tables of the loan sharks, spilling coins left and right.
MSG.16: He told the dove merchants, "Get your things out of here! Stop turning my Father's house into a shopping mall!"
MSG.17: That's when his disciples remembered the Scripture, "Zeal for your house consumes me."
MSG.18: But the Jews were upset. They asked, "What credentials can you present to justify this?"
MSG.19: Jesus answered, "Tear down this Temple and in three days I'll put it back together."
MSG.20: They were indignant: "It took forty-six years to build this Temple, and you're going to rebuild it in three days?"
MSG.21: But Jesus was talking about his body as the Temple.
MSG.22: Later, after he was raised from the dead, his disciples remembered he had said this. They then put two and two together and believed both what was written in Scripture and what Jesus had said.
MSG.23: During the time he was in Jerusalem, those days of the Passover Feast, many people noticed the signs he was displaying and, seeing they pointed straight to God, entrusted their lives to him.
MSG.24: But Jesus didn't entrust his life to them. He knew them inside and out, knew how untrustworthy they were.
MSG.25: He didn't need any help in seeing right through them.

NIV.1:
Jesus Changes Water to Wine
On the third day a wedding took place at Cana in Galilee. Jesus’ mother was there,
NIV.2: and Jesus and his disciples had also been invited to the wedding.
NIV.3: When the wine was gone, Jesus’ mother said to him, “They have no more wine.”
NIV.4: “Dear woman, why do you involve me?” Jesus replied. “My time has not yet come.”
NIV.5: His mother said to the servants, “Do whatever he tells you.”
NIV.6: Nearby stood six stone water jars, the kind used by the Jews for ceremonial washing, each holding from twenty to thirty gallons.
NIV.7: Jesus said to the servants, “Fill the jars with water”; so they filled them to the brim.
NIV.8:
Then he told them, “Now draw some out and take it to the master of the banquet.”
They did so,
NIV.9: and the master of the banquet tasted the water that had been turned into wine. He did not realize where it had come from, though the servants who had drawn the water knew. Then he called the bridegroom aside
NIV.10: and said, “Everyone brings out the choice wine first and then the cheaper wine after the guests have had too much to drink; but you have saved the best till now.”
NIV.11: This, the first of his miraculous signs, Jesus performed at Cana in Galilee. He thus revealed his glory, and his disciples put their faith in him.
NIV.12:
Jesus Clears the Temple
After this he went down to Capernaum with his mother and brothers and his disciples. There they stayed for a few days.
NIV.13: When it was almost time for the Jewish Passover, Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
NIV.14: In the temple courts he found men selling cattle, sheep and doves, and others sitting at tables exchanging money.
NIV.15: So he made a whip out of cords, and drove all from the temple area, both sheep and cattle; he scattered the coins of the money changers and overturned their tables.
NIV.16: To those who sold doves he said, “Get these out of here! How dare you turn my Father’s house into a market!”
NIV.17: His disciples remembered that it is written: “Zeal for your house will consume me.”
NIV.18: Then the Jews demanded of him, “What miraculous sign can you show us to prove your authority to do all this?”
NIV.19: Jesus answered them, “Destroy this temple, and I will raise it again in three days.”
NIV.20: The Jews replied, “It has taken forty-six years to build this temple, and you are going to raise it in three days?”
NIV.21: But the temple he had spoken of was his body.
NIV.22: After he was raised from the dead, his disciples recalled what he had said. Then they believed the Scripture and the words that Jesus had spoken.
NIV.23: Now while he was in Jerusalem at the Passover Feast, many people saw the miraculous signs he was doing and believed in his name.
NIV.24: But Jesus would not entrust himself to them, for he knew all men.
NIV.25: He did not need man’s testimony about man, for he knew what was in a man.

NRSVCE.1: On the third day there was a wedding in Cana of Galilee, and the mother of Jesus was there.
NRSVCE.2: Jesus and his disciples had also been invited to the wedding.
NRSVCE.3: When the wine gave out, the mother of Jesus said to him, “They have no wine.”
NRSVCE.4: And Jesus said to her, “Woman, what concern is that to you and to me? My hour has not yet come.”
NRSVCE.5: His mother said to the servants, “Do whatever he tells you.”
NRSVCE.6: Now standing there were six stone water jars for the Jewish rites of purification, each holding twenty or thirty gallons.
NRSVCE.7: Jesus said to them, “Fill the jars with water.” And they filled them up to the brim.
NRSVCE.8: He said to them, “Now draw some out, and take it to the chief steward.” So they took it.
NRSVCE.9: When the steward tasted the water that had become wine, and did not know where it came from (though the servants who had drawn the water knew), the steward called the bridegroom
NRSVCE.10: and said to him, “Everyone serves the good wine first, and then the inferior wine after the guests have become drunk. But you have kept the good wine until now.”
NRSVCE.11: Jesus did this, the first of his signs, in Cana of Galilee, and revealed his glory; and his disciples believed in him.
NRSVCE.12: After this he went down to Capernaum with his mother, his brothers, and his disciples; and they remained there a few days.
NRSVCE.13: The Passover of the Jews was near, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
NRSVCE.14: In the temple he found people selling cattle, sheep, and doves, and the money changers seated at their tables.
NRSVCE.15: Making a whip of cords, he drove all of them out of the temple, both the sheep and the cattle. He also poured out the coins of the money changers and overturned their tables.
NRSVCE.16: He told those who were selling the doves, “Take these things out of here! Stop making my Father’s house a marketplace!”
NRSVCE.17: His disciples remembered that it was written, “Zeal for your house will consume me.”
NRSVCE.18: The Jews then said to him, “What sign can you show us for doing this?”
NRSVCE.19: Jesus answered them, “Destroy this temple, and in three days I will raise it up.”
NRSVCE.20: The Jews then said, “This temple has been under construction for forty-six years, and will you raise it up in three days?”
NRSVCE.21: But he was speaking of the temple of his body.
NRSVCE.22: After he was raised from the dead, his disciples remembered that he had said this; and they believed the scripture and the word that Jesus had spoken.
NRSVCE.23: When he was in Jerusalem during the Passover festival, many believed in his name because they saw the signs that he was doing.
NRSVCE.24: But Jesus on his part would not entrust himself to them, because he knew all people
NRSVCE.25: and needed no one to testify about anyone; for he himself knew what was in everyone.

NET.1: Now on the third day there was a wedding at Cana in Galilee. Jesus’ mother was there,
NET.2: and Jesus and his disciples were also invited to the wedding.
NET.3: When the wine ran out, Jesus’ mother said to him, “They have no wine left.”
NET.4: Jesus replied, “Woman, why are you saying this to me? My time has not yet come.”
NET.5: His mother told the servants, “Whatever he tells you, do it.”
NET.6: Now there were six stone water jars there for Jewish ceremonial washing, each holding twenty or thirty gallons.
NET.7: Jesus told the servants, “Fill the water jars with water.” So they filled them up to the very top.
NET.8: Then he told them, “Now draw some out and take it to the head steward,” and they did.
NET.9: When the head steward tasted the water that had been turned to wine, not knowing where it came from (though the servants who had drawn the water knew), he called the bridegroom
NET.10: and said to him, “Everyone serves the good wine first, and then the cheaper wine when the guests are drunk. You have kept the good wine until now!”
NET.11: Jesus did this as the first of his miraculous signs, in Cana of Galilee. In this way he revealed his glory, and his disciples believed in him.
NET.12: After this he went down to Capernaum with his mother and brothers and his disciples, and they stayed there a few days.
NET.13: Now the Jewish feast of Passover was near, so Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
NET.14: He found in the temple courts those who were selling oxen and sheep and doves, and the money changers sitting at tables.
NET.15: So he made a whip of cords and drove them all out of the temple courts, with the sheep and the oxen. He scattered the coins of the money changers and overturned their tables.
NET.16: To those who sold the doves he said, “Take these things away from here! Do not make my Father’s house a marketplace!”
NET.17: His disciples remembered that it was written, “Zeal for your house will devour me.”
NET.18: So then the Jewish leaders responded, “What sign can you show us, since you are doing these things?”
NET.19: Jesus replied, “Destroy this temple and in three days I will raise it up again.”
NET.20: Then the Jewish leaders said to him, “This temple has been under construction for forty-six years, and are you going to raise it up in three days?”
NET.21: But Jesus was speaking about the temple of his body.
NET.22: So after he was raised from the dead, his disciples remembered that he had said this, and they believed the scripture and the saying that Jesus had spoken.
NET.23: Now while Jesus was in Jerusalem at the feast of the Passover, many people believed in his name because they saw the miraculous signs he was doing.
NET.24: But Jesus would not entrust himself to them, because he knew all people.
NET.25: He did not need anyone to testify about man, for he knew what was in man.
